meaning: n.閲嶆柊浼拌,閲嶆柊璇勪环
examples: The state's worst prison riot will force a fundamental reappraisal of prison policy.
The dotcom and housing bubbles led to a reappraisal of Mr Greenspan's career.
This is not going to be a little recessionary dip. It will be a more fundamental reappraisal.
The timing for the celebration and reappraisal of Mies's work could not be better.
If rates rise elsewhere, he said, a "reappraisal" of the tactic might be needed.
